content.text: How do I dynamically ensure that a specific method within a class is internally synchronized or wrapped every time it's called?
If a class already has a method named synchronize, what happens when this decorator tries to use it?

The implementation relies on the assumption that `self.synchronize` exists and is a callable (usually another decorator or wrapper) capable of handling the dynamic decoration process. If it doesn't exist, an AttributeError will occur.
This is quite clever for runtime modification!
